key_points: Dorms described as workable but beds require tricks to assemble
One student moved out after academic/ethics trouble first semester
No strong complaints about housing quality in the data
key_quotes: get a camping mallet and use that to get the post to dislodge from its connection
had to move out of my dorm and back in with my parents

key_points: Making friends is genuinely hard; some students feel isolated and judged
Campus pranks happen — soap in the fountain outside the cafe noted
Not a nightlife school; Greensboro described as 'not really a real city'
key_quotes: The people scare me on campus and I'm completely alone. I haven't made friends and people judge me.
UNCG has a bit more going on but isn't really a real city
somebody put soap in the fountain outside the cafe so be prepared to see some shit

key_points: Food described as good both on and off campus
Small diners off Walker Ave noted for students near nursing or media buildings
Vegan students express anxiety about meal plan options
key_quotes: The food is good, on and off campus, there's great little diners off of Walker if you're down that way for nursing or media, theater
I'm coming in as a freshmen, and a vegan. I'm pretty nervous about my meal plan

key_points: At least one student got kicked out after severe academic struggles first year
Social isolation is a real issue — one student explicitly said they were completely alone
No mention of counseling services or mental health resources in student data
key_quotes: just 4 years ago I was a freshman at UNC Greensboro and got kicked out due to my bad grades after the first two semesters
The people scare me on campus and I'm completely alone. I haven't made friends and people judge me.

key_points: In-state cost around $18,000/year — considered affordable among NC public schools
Students use UNCG as a stepping stone while saving money before transferring
No merit aid mentioned; students with other offers compare UNCG as the cheap fallback
key_quotes: I think UNC Greensboro I would be paying like $18,000
I got a $159,000 merit scholarship for Wooster so I would be paying roughly $29,000 a year... is Wooster worth the extra money?

key_points: Professors praised as knowledgeable, approachable, and genuinely invested in students
Music program considered stronger and more renowned than App State's
CS program available but seen as inferior to NC State by those who compared
key_quotes: no matter what subject, they know their stuff, they're easy to talk to, and want to help you succeed
the UNCG music program is (IMO) better and more renown than App's
NCSU has a much better program than UNCG

key_points: Academic probation and suspension process exists and has affected students
Rescission of admission seen as unlikely even with poor senior year grades
Transfer process from community college appears straightforward
key_quotes: U won't get rescinded from uncg dont worry lol
just 4 years ago I was a freshman at UNC Greensboro and got kicked out due to my bad grades after the first two semesters. I had to go through all the Academic Probation and Suspension

key_points: Campus called scenic, beautiful, and safe to walk at night
Greensboro is not a major city — limited nightlife compared to larger metros
Medium-sized campus — big enough to explore, not overwhelming
key_quotes: UNC Greensboro has one of the most scenic and beautiful campuses I have ever seen
you feel safe walking at night from evening classes
UNCG has a bit more going on but isn't really a real city

key_points: No strong data on job placement; online students question networking opportunities
Pre-pharmacy and pre-med students use UNCG as a launchpad to grad school
UNCG degree seen as less prestigious than NC State for competitive fields like CS
key_quotes: Are they good about networking, job placement, etc.?
NCSU has a much better program than UNCG and you'll probably have better opportunities
I'd go UNC Greensboro. No one's gonna see that on your resume and think 'I really don't want to deal with that'

key_points: Strong value for in-state students at ~$18k/year
Frequently used as an affordable transfer pathway or backup to Chapel Hill
Out-of-state students warned to weigh cost carefully against more prestigious options
key_quotes: I think UNC Greensboro I would be paying like $18,000
His goal is to go to med school... the cost of college will be roughly 3-4 times the cost of going to Greensboro
I got in and committed to the University of North Carolina in Greensboro. It's close to home and I plan to transfer to UNC Chapel Hill after two years.

key_points: Solid regional school with a warm academic community but weak social scene
Often chosen for practicality — affordability, proximity, transfer plans — not passion
Students who engage find it fine; those who struggle socially find it isolating
key_quotes: I really loved the campus and the teachers
The people scare me on campus and I'm completely alone. I haven't made friends and people judge me.
It's close to home and I plan to transfer to UNC Chapel Hill after two years

key_points: **Social Isolation:** multiple students report loneliness and feeling judged on campus
**Academic Attrition:** students dismissed after first year for poor grades with little cushion
**Perception:** frequently listed as a last-resort safety or a transfer stepping stone, not a destination
key_quotes: The people scare me on campus and I'm completely alone. I haven't made friends and people judge me.
just 4 years ago I was a freshman at UNC Greensboro and got kicked out due to my bad grades
I got in and committed to UNCG... I plan to transfer to UNC Chapel Hill after two years

key_points: Music program is legitimately strong — outranks nearby App State among students who compared
First Year Experience class praised as 'amazing and truly helpful' by current student
Walker Ave off-campus dining scene offers real local food options beyond the dining hall
key_quotes: the UNCG music program is (IMO) better and more renown than App's
I would recommend signing up for the First Year Experience class! It is amazing and truly helpful!
there's great little diners off of Walker if you're down that way for nursing or media, theater
